channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The new Apple MacBook Air M4 has a weird quirk with its performance cores - but it's nothing to worry about
item.description: If you've been using Adobe Lightroom Classic with the new Apple MacBook Air (M4), you might want to have a look at your CPU usage.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Emily in Paris season 5: everything we know so far about the hit Netflix show’s return
item.description: Emily in Paris season 5 begins filming in May – here's everything we know about the hit Netflix show's return so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung is offering you 8 new TVs in one bundle for March Madness, in case you want to watch all games at once like a Bond villain’s lair
item.description: In a first-of-its-kind deal, Samsung is selling you eight different TVs in one bundle so you can catch every moment of NNCA's March Madness tournament.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: The latest iPhone 17 Pro Max leak may have given us another look at its upcoming redesign
item.description: We've got some more evidence showing the upcoming redesign – and it sounds as though production is on schedule.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why thinking like a tech company is essential for your business’s survival
item.description: To thrive in today’s business environment, companies must align their technological and cultural foundations with their ultimate goals.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel reveals its new CEO
item.description: Intel has gained a new CEO three months after Pat Gelsinger announced he’d be leaving – Lip-Bu Tan to take post.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices but US remains hesitant
item.description: Vaccines could be a key means of suppressing bird flu and avoiding the slaughter of millions of chickens, which is blamed for egg prices averaging nearly $5 a dozen.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google unveils Gemma 3 multi-modal AI models
item.description: Google DeepMind has introduced Gemma 3, an update to the company’s family of generative AI models, featuring multi-modality that allows the models to analyze images, answer questions about images, identify objects, and perform other tasks that involve analyzing and understanding visual data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Majority of Americans have used AI models like ChatGPT: Survey
item.description: A majority of Americans have used ChatGPT-like artificial intelligence (AI) models according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Intel taps semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as new CEO
item.description: Intel today announced its new CEO is Lip-Bu Tan, a semiconductor industry veteran who has focused on software but nonetheless is very familiar with Intel. Tan previously was CEO of Cadence Design Systems, which makes software products used to design and verify complex electronic systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How the Trump administration plans to use algorithms to target protesters
item.description: As protests against the Trump administration and in favor of Palestine continue to grow across the country, the U.S. State Department is reportedly planning to use tech to try and tamp down on dissent.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ServiceNow’s Yokohama platform release focuses on agentic AI
item.description: ServiceNow today launched its newest Now Platform release, Yokohama, which doubles down on the company’s commitment to agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2 tasty new tab tricks for Chrome on Android
item.description: Quick: How many tabs do you have open in Chrome on your Android phone right now? If the answer is zero, you’re an animal. Seek immediate psychiatric help. If the answer is one to two, you’re obsessively organized and a model Android device owner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ServiceNow to pay $2.85B for Moveworks’ AI tools
item.description: ServiceNow announced plans to purchase Moveworks, its front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: What to expect from the AI leader
item.description: No company has both driven and benefited from AI advancements more than Nvidia. Last year, Nvidia’s GTC 2024 grabbed headlines with its introduction of the Blackwell architecture and the DGX systems powered by it. With GTC 2025 right around the corner (it runs March 17- 21 in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
